title: Patch/document Carbonite restore-upload behavior and pre-restore runtime detection
description:
## Why
The active integration restore bead covers performing and validating the live restore on my-assistant-v7, but newly requested work also includes Carbonite source/doc changes: patching and documenting the new restore-upload behavior and investigating Carbonite install location, PATH assumptions, and runtime-shape changes before restore.

## What
Track the clawrbonite-repo work needed to:
- patch/document restore-upload behavior
- investigate Carbonite install location and PATH/runtime-shape assumptions before restore
- capture any repo-local follow-up needed for Carbonite restore robustness

## Relationship
This supports integration bead integration-ops-ovm.6 but belongs in the clawrbonite repo because it is repo-local Carbonite behavior/documentation work, not only live integration execution.
notes: 2026-04-26 added sandbox-local GITHUB_TOKEN fallback for Carbonite git transport. scripts/carbonite-init.sh and scripts/carbonite-backup.sh now source ~/.openclaw-data/carbonite/env.sh when present and, if that file exports a real materialized GITHUB_TOKEN instead of an OpenShell placeholder, they use a one-shot in-memory Basic auth header for git clone/fetch/push without persisting credentials into git config. scripts/env-setup.sh and the deployment guide now document the fallback. Deployed updated scripts into my-assistant-v7, wrote ~/.openclaw-data/carbonite/env.sh with the temporary token workaround, and validated a live backup push succeeded as commit cbbccdd post-restore auth-fallback validation.

title: Make Carbonite cron jobs PATH-safe
close_reason: Completed: cron setup now registers the executable Carbonite backup helper by absolute path, avoiding dependence on interactive shell PATH setup; deployment guide reflects the helper path.

title: Ignore QMD cache/model artifacts in Carbonite backups
close_reason: Completed: Carbonite now excludes QMD xdg-cache/xdg-config runtime cache, index, and model artifacts in generated gitignore and backup/init untracking paths; deployment guide documents post-restore rebuild expectations.

title: Update websearch helper for current Carbonite runtime shape
description:
## Why
The restored websearch helper may still assume older sandbox-home layout or PATH conventions that no longer hold under the current NemoClaw runtime model.

## What
Review and update the archived websearch helper and related docs/invocation assumptions for the current writable-root and runtime-managed shell model.

## Outcomes
- websearch path/runtime assumptions are documented
- any required helper or archive-shape changes are tracked separately from the core Carbonite refactor
- restore planning can proceed without bundling websearch cleanup into the main Carbonite design decision

title: Update ghwatch for current Carbonite runtime/auth model
description:
## Why
The current Carbonite/NemoClaw runtime model has shifted around writable paths, shell init ownership, and GitHub CLI mediated auth. The restored ghwatch helper likely still assumes older sandbox-home conventions or direct token/env behavior.

## What
Review and update the archived ghwatch helper and any related docs or invocation assumptions so it fits the current writable/runtime-owned layout and provider-backed GitHub auth model.

## Outcomes
- ghwatch path/runtime assumptions are documented
- required code or archive-shape follow-up is tracked separately from the core Carbonite refactor
- restore planning is not blocked on unresolved ghwatch drift

title: Investigate stale Carbonite backup lock after successful cron run
description: After the fresh rebuild/restore, sandbox-local Carbonite cron backups appear healthy (lastRunStatus ok and new backup commits exist), but manual carbonite-backup still reports "Another backup is already running. Skipping." even when no obvious backup/git/flock process is visible from a sandbox-side /proc probe. Investigate whether the lock is genuinely held by a lingering process, whether the sandbox lacks enough process-inspection tooling to see the holder, or whether the lock handling in carbonite-backup has a stale-holder edge case. Preserve current healthy backup behavior while diagnosing; do not blindly delete ~/.carbonite.lock without confirming the holder state.
notes: Confirmed root-cause clue from host-side /proc probe: the Carbonite lock was not held by an active backup, but by an orphaned interactive `/bin/bash -i` inside the sandbox (`PPid: 1`) with fd 9 open on `/sandbox/.carbonite.lock`. A normal `kill` did not clear it; `kill -9` on the holder PID was required before manual `carbonite-backup` could run again. After killing the holder, manual backup `post-carbonite fix backup` succeeded and pushed commit `a001d36`. This points to a likely OpenShell `sandbox connect` session-cleanup leak (user also observed unattended connect sessions ending with broken pipe), not a Carbonite backup logic failure by itself.

title: Preserve canonical memory continuity in backup/init flow
notes: Updated Carbonite backup/init contract to preserve canonical OpenClaw memory continuity from .openclaw-data/memory instead of the facade path under .openclaw/. The generated .gitignore now ignores the facade tree wholesale again, stage_preserved_paths in both scripts now includes .openclaw-data/memory, and the deployment guide now documents canonical memory preservation while keeping facade/runtime-integration paths out of scope. Extensions and secret-bearing env files remain excluded by design.
close_reason: Completed. Carbonite now preserves canonical OpenClaw memory continuity from .openclaw-data/memory while leaving facade paths, extensions, and secret-bearing runtime env files outside the backup contract. Scripts and deployment guide were updated, bash syntax checks passed, and the clawrbonite repo change was committed and pushed.
